title: Cancer: A bioelectric disease?
snippet: Cancer progression is driven by coordinated alterations in signalling networks that regulate proliferation, plasticity, metabolism and therapeutic response. Although genetic and epigenetic mechanisms are well characterised, there is an increasing body of evidence that suggests bioelectric signalling constitutes an additional integrative regulatory layer in....

title: Diagnostic Accuracy and Reproducibility of ChatGPT-4o for HER2 Immunohistochemistry Scoring in Equivocal Breast Cancer Cases.
publicationName: Asian Pacific journal of cancer prevention : APJCP
journalName: Asian Pacific journal of cancer prevention : APJCP
snippet: HER2 immunohistochemistry (IHC) plays a central role in therapeutic decision-making for breast cancer. However, interpretation of equivocal (2+) IHC results remains challenging and is subject to interobserver variability, necessitating reflex in situ hybridization testing. This study evaluated the diagnostic performance and reproducibility of ChatGPT-4o, a....

title: Macrophages in Psoriasis: From Functional Heterogeneity to Metabolic Reprogramming and Novel Therapeutics.
snippet: This review provides a comprehensive overview of macrophage heterogeneity, polarisation dynamics, metabolic reprogramming and multicellular interactions of macrophages in psoriasis. We discuss how single-cell technologies have revealed diverse macrophage subsets and explore the metabolic profile of macrophages in psoriasis. Furthermore, we examine the centr...

title: MRI prognostic features in rectal cancer neoadjuvant trials: A systematic review of reporting gaps across two decades.
publicationName: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland
journalName: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland
snippet: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is central to staging and treatment planning in rectal cancer, particularly in identifying candidates for neoadjuvant therapy. Prognostic MRI features such as tumour depth (mrT), extramural venous invasion (EMVI) and tumour deposits (TDs) are increasingly recognised as markers of systemic risk, yet their reporting in randomi...
